title: Deterministic rule extractor (age, perf-status, labs, washout, negation)
context: The deterministic rule extractor is the preferred, reproducible path for converting eligibility free-text into structured assertions, ahead of any LLM assist. It handles common patterns (age, performance status, lab thresholds, washout windows, prior-therapy negations) with unit and temporality. Ambiguous criteria must never be auto-completed; they route to the human-must-resolve queue and are withheld from release.
objective: Build a deterministic rule/grammar extractor that converts common eligibility statements into schema-valid assertions with unit, temporality, and negation, and routes ambiguous criteria to human-must-resolve.
acceptanceCriteria: Deterministic extraction of common patterns (age, ECOG/Lansky/Karnofsky, lab thresholds, washout windows, prior-therapy negations) with unit + temporality + negation handling.
Age-appropriate performance status: Lansky for <16, Karnofsky/ECOG for >=16, with the scale recorded.
No criterion is auto-marked complete if ambiguous; it is routed to human-must-resolve and withheld.
Extractor output validates against the eligibility schema and is deterministic/reproducible from a clean clone.
